Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Initial load test data and latency targets

Hi Irene / Ben / Monica,

Thanks again for the call this morning. As discussed, we're read...
text:
From: Samir Gupta <samir@concord.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Initial load test data and latency targets

Hi Irene / Ben / Monica,

Thanks again for the call this morning. As discussed, we're ready to run the first full acceptance pass for Concord.ai's query service. Below are the primary acceptance targets we need to validate during the onboarding run: 

- Throughput: sustained 120 requests/sec (avg) for 30 minutes with standard 50% query mix
- P95 latency: < 180 ms end-to-end
- Tail (99): < 500 ms (traffic bursts allowed during the first 10 minutes)
- Error budget: fewer than 0.5% 5xx or timeouts across run

I've attached the sample load profile and the dataset split we'll use (concord_onboard_load_plan.csv). We can run the test window any time during 2027-06-02 o...

preview:

To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@hearthlytics.com>, Hearthlytics Procurement <procurement@hearthlytics.com>

text:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@hearthlytics.com>, Hearthlytics Procurement <procurement@hearthlytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 29 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Per-million price proposal and benchmarks

Hi Liam / Hearthlytics team — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick written recap and next steps so we’re aligned: 

Summary of where Hearthlytics landed on requirements:
- Primary workload: high-volume generative prompts (completion tokens) for customer-facing features + occasional embedding workloads for semantic search.
- Expected monthly consumption: 12M–25M generated tokens in month 1, scaling to 100M tokens/month by Q4 if pilot goes well.
- Target latency SLO: p95 < 450ms for short prompts; tolerance for higher latency on long-form jobs.

Hi Ava,

text:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Singh <ava@hypersynth.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, May 14, 2027 at 10:04 AM PDT
Subject: Gradient commit curve proposal for Hypersynth - initial model

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the time today. Attached is the Excel model I promised — it lays out a 'gradient commit curve' where Hypersynth ramps GPU and TPM commitments over 18 months rather than a single front-loaded tranche. Key highlights:

- Year 1: 40% of committed GPUs / TPM as an annual prepay with 8% term discount
- Year 2: additional 30% (paid at start of year) -> incremental discount step to 10%
- Year 3: final 30% with 12% total term discount on the aggregate commit if benchmarks (throughput/latency) are met

We also show two failure modes in tab 3: underutilization (where you pay for spare capacity) and acceleration credits (if you accelerate ramp, a credit is...

From: Emily Tran <emily@lumendata.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-22T09:18:00-07:00
Subject: Quick ask — cohort promo credits for our 'Data Tacticians' mini-series

Hi Avery,

Hope you\t\t\t

We\tre running a three-session mini-series next month for our Data Tacticians community (short panels + hands-on quickstarts). We\td like to offer 150 attendees a complimentary 2000-token trial top-up (to cover post-demo experimentation).

Would Redwood be open to a small promo allocation (total ~300k tokens) and a formalized SOW so we can list Redwood as a platform partner on the event page? We can surface the Redwood logo and a short blurb.

Timing: sessions June 8/15/22 — tight turnaround, so hoping to finalize credits & SOW this week.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 20-min call if that helps.

Best,
Emily
Emily Tran
Partnerships Lead, LumenData

text:
From: Asha Raman <asha.raman@steelforge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick sync: marketplace listing + integration dependency

Ben —

Thanks again for the call this week. We scoped the technical work on our side and there are two items that will block the Cloud Marketplace pre-list gating:

- License entitlement verification flow (our SSO returns org.id only; marketplace needs tokenized entitlement proof)
- Deployment packaging: SteelForge delivers a containerized adaptor that needs a dedicated IAM role when deployed into the marketplace staging account

We want to understand if Redwood can grant a temporary entitlement exception for the early-ship, while we complete the entitlement flow (we estimate 4–6 weeks). Also need a short checklist of the security artifacts your marketplace team will require to approve packaging.

Ca...

Hi Sam / Jorge —

Thanks for the time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum:

- Action: Share MAP draft with milestone owners (attached: Acme_MAP_v1.docx).
- Legal milestone: Acme to share initial redlines; Redwood to assign internal owner for counter-redlines.
- Target close: Customer indicated desire to finalize MSA & DPA by June 18 (internal target: 2027-06-15).
- Dependencies: SOC 2 report request, KMS integration notes (Sam will share contact).

Hi Ava,

Thanks for participating in our RFP. A couple of follow-ups from our legal and security teams around loggin...
text:
From: Jane Doe <jane.doe@finco.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: security-gov@finco.com
Date: 2027-05-21T09:12:00Z
Subject: RFI: Cross-region retention, exportability and restore operations

Hi Ava,

Thanks for participating in our RFP. A couple of follow-ups from our legal and security teams around logging/audit retention and exportability:

1) Retention windows: what retention buckets do you support (default and customizable options)? Can retention be set per-tenant, per-route, or per-index?

2) Cross-region retention/replication: if we require data residency in multiple regions (us-east-1 and eu-west-1), how do you guarantee exportability of the full audit trail for a given tenant and time range?

3) Bulk export & restore SLA: expected timelines to produce a bulk extract (raw audit events + manifests) and turnaround for a restore into our environment. Any expedited option...

text:
From: Alex Cheng <alex.cheng@pulselytics.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@pulselytics.ai
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Hosted API pricing inquiry and possible pilot

Ben —

Thanks again for the demo this week. The team at Pulselytics is excited about Redwood's performance characteristics for our streaming ingestion patterns. We ran a quick internal model and expect ~1.2B tokens/month in steady state with a 6–9 month ramp.

Two asks from procurement before we move to PO:
- Can you provide a 12-month committed rate for Hosted API that reflects volume (we're exploring a committed plan).
- We're requesting a one-time commercial concession: a 22% unit-rate discount for the first 6 months to help bridge our ramp risk. This is higher than standard published tiers, but if accepted we would commit to a 12-month reserved billing schedule and include a ...
